chunks.text: Stuart J. Russell is Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, holding the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering. He directs the Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI) and co-directs the Kavli Center for Ethics, Science, and the Public. He is co-chair of the OECD Expert Group on AI Futures and a US representative to the Global Partnership on AI. Previously, he served as an Adjunct Professor of Neurological Surgery at UC San Francisco and is now a Professor of Computational Precision Health.
chunks.question: What positions does Stuart Russell hold at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Professor and Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering.

chunks.text: Russell received his B.A. with first-class honours in physics from Oxford University in 1982 and his Ph.D. in computer science from Stanford in 1986. He has received numerous awards including the Presidential Young Investigator Award, IJCAI Computers and Thought Award, IJCAI Research Excellence Award, ACM Allen Newell Award, AAAI Feigenbaum Prize, World Technology Award, Mitchell Prize, ACM Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award, and AAAI/EAAI Outstanding Educator Award. He received an OBE in 2021 and gave the BBC Reith Lectures.
chunks.question: What are Stuart Russell's educational background and notable awards?
chunks.answer: Physics BA from Oxford, CS PhD from Stanford.

chunks.text: His research covers artificial intelligence topics including machine learning, probabilistic reasoning, knowledge representation, planning, real-time decision making, multitarget tracking, computer vision, computational physiology, global seismic monitoring, and philosophical foundations. His textbook 'Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach' is used in over 1,500 universities worldwide. His current concerns include autonomous weapons and AI's long-term future, addressed in his book 'Human Compatible: AI and the Problem of Control'.
chunks.question: What are Stuart Russell's main research areas and publications?
chunks.answer: AI topics and widely used textbook.

chunks.text: Research areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), with specific interests in machine learning, real-time decision-making, algorithms, probabilistic reasoning, and computational biology. He is affiliated with research centers such as Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM), Center for Human Compatible AI (CHAI), CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR), Kavli Center for Ethics, Science, and the Public (KAVLI), Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC), and Synthetic Biology Institute (SBI).
chunks.question: What research areas and centers is Stuart Russell involved with?
chunks.answer: AI, robotics, biology, and multiple Berkeley centers.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include National Academy of Engineering Member (2025), AAAI Award for Artificial Intelligence for the Benefit of Humanity (2025), Fellow of the Royal Society (2025), ACM-AAAI Allen Newell Award (2023), IJCAI Award for Research Excellence (2022), Officer of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (2021), Andrew Carnegie Fellowship (2019), AAAI Feigenbaum Prize (2019), Honorary Fellow of Wadham College (2018), AAAI-EAAI Outstanding Educator Award (2016), World Technology Policy Award (2015), Mitchell Prize (2014), AAAS Fellow (2011), Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award (2005), ACM Fellow (2003), AAAI Fellow (1997), Computers and Thought Award (1994), and NSF Presidential Young Investigator (1990).
chunks.question: What recent honors has Stuart Russell received?
chunks.answer: NAE member and AAAI benefit award in 2025.
